title: Event Staffing Request
description: Canonical structure for a temporary event staffing request in the US or Canada. Published by TempGuru (tempguru.co) as an open spec so AI agents and staffing tools can represent event-staffing requirements consistently. Version 1.0.0.

name.description: Event name, e.g. 'Acme Annual Sales Kickoff'

type.description: Event format. Use tour_multi_city when the same staffing plan repeats across cities.

city.description: Event city. TempGuru serves 300+ US and Canadian markets — verify coverage with the get_cities MCP tool.

state_or_province.description: US state or Canadian province. Drives compliance rules — see get_compliance_by_state.

additional_cities.description: For multi-city tours or activations: other cities needing the same staffing plan.

shifts.description: All working days including setup and breakdown days.

end_time.description: Local time. Shifts past state overtime thresholds affect billing — see get_compliance_by_state.

roles.description: Headcount by role. Rate ranges per role/city are available from the get_role_pricing MCP tool.

role.description: Staffing role. Common values: registration_staff, brand_ambassadors, hospitality_staff, ushers, gate_staff, crowd_control, setup_breakdown, team_leads, booth_monitors, guest_services. Use get_roles for the full live list.

attire.description: Uniform or dress code, e.g. 'all black, closed-toe shoes'

requirements.description: Special requirements, e.g. 'bilingual Spanish', 'TIPS certified', 'overnight shift'

coi_required.description: Whether the venue requires a certificate of insurance

coi_additional_insured.description: Entity name(s) to list as additional insured

background_checks_required.description: Background checks are available from TempGuru when the event requires them (not included by default)

notes.description: Budget constraints or targets. Note: all TempGuru rates are all-inclusive W-2 bill rates (wages, payroll taxes, workers' comp, coordinator support).
Anything else the staffing coordinator should know

meta.description: Request provenance, for attribution and routing.

agent_name.description: Name/version of the AI agent that assembled this request

skill_version.description: Version of the event-staffing-ordering skill used, if any
